title: Celtis sumatrana
intro: Celtis sumatrana, also known as the Sumatran hackberry, is a plant species in the family Cannabaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The health benefits of Celtis sumatrana include its use as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional medicine, Celtis sumatrana is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Celtis sumatrana is classified in the family Ulmaceae, commonly known as the elm family. This family includes various species of trees and shrubs, some of which are native to Asia and others to Europe. Celtis sumatrana, a tropical species, is native to Southeast Asia and is often found in tropical forests and woodlands. The species is a member of the genus Celtis, which comprises about 60 to 70 species of deciduous and evergreen trees and shrubs. Celtis sumatrana is further classified as Celtis sumatrana Teijsm. & Binn.
uses: Celtis sumatrana, a plant native to Southeast Asia, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. The extracts from this plant have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which could be beneficial in treating various health conditions. In traditional medicine, Celtis sumatrana is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and bark are also used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. benefits: Celtis sumatrana, commonly known as the Sumatran hackberry or Sumatran nettle tree, has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its various health benefits. Its leaves and bark are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain.
